_comment: The size ratchet for PRODUCT_SPEC.md (SPEC INV-264). The bound is bytes-per-criterion: the byte count of the spec's criterion lines alone (glossary and preamble excluded) divided by the count of criteria in its body. A delivery may lower it or leave it; no delivery may raise it (raising is a change to Requirement 4, INV-265). The ratchet governs PRODUCT_SPEC.md only; the other documents keep their flat byte bound (check-doc-bound.py).
reason: Seeded at the row-445 spec-format migration-end freeze and lowered by the freeze actor at the pass-2 re-baseline (206.9 over 1360 criteria; the pass-1 seed was 209.0 over 1301). Re-baselined up at the row-456 architecture-format landing to 207.2 (measured 207.196 over 1420 criteria): six behavioral rules relocated from ARCHITECTURE.md owns cells into their spec clauses (INV-279) — the runaway-child fire-timing, the perms-arm settings scope and glob/spaced-path parsing and unreadable stand-down, the hedge-gate personal overlay, the read-grant profile field, and the node-growth and expensive-decision push status — plus the three architecture-format requirements 289-291. The companion move shed 31 KB from ARCHITECTURE.md, so the system's total content fell while the spec's criterion density rose. This raise is the deliberate Requirement-4 change INV-265 requires; a later delivery may lower this bound or leave it, and none may raise it without the same justification (INV-264, INV-265).
